title: Alex Cora identified 2 players he views as leaders of the 2026 Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora named Trevor Story and Garrett Crochet as leaders of the 2026 Red Sox team.
Story led the Red Sox in home runs (25) and RBIs (96) during his first healthy season since 2022.
Garrett Crochet, a key starting pitcher, led MLB in strikeouts with 255 and was named the Red Sox's Opening Day starter.

title: 4 things to watch during Boston Legacy FC’s NWSL debut against Gotham FC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC makes its NWSL debut against Gotham FC at Gillette Stadium on March 14, 2026.
The game will be played on newly-installed grass, possibly aiding Boston's possession-focused strategy.
Gotham FC, the defending NWSL champions, features stars like Rose Lavelle and Esther González.

title: Should Massachusetts stop changing its clocks?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers debate ending the twice-yearly clock change, with support for legislation moving to permanent time.
Senate President Karen Spilka supports S.2157, citing disruptions to routines and potential public health benefits.
Research links time changes to higher risks of health issues, though it may affect New England's daylight hours.

title: Boston Pride For The People announces 2026 parade and festival date
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston Pride For The People has announced a parade and festival on Saturday, June 6, 2026.
The 2026 parade starts at 11 a.m. in Copley Square, ending at Boston Common with a festival and concert.
Mayor Michelle Wu praised the event's inclusivity, and volunteers are being recruited via the group's website.

title: Feds release teen detained by ICE in Marlborough
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A 14-year-old Brazilian girl detained by ICE in Marlborough was released following a federal judge's order on Thursday.
The girl was initially detained by federal agents who claimed she was rescued from gang members, per a DHS spokesperson.
Her lawyer will petition for legal residency, as she's been issued a notice to appear in immigration court after her release.

title: With ‘top guys’ struggling, a lineup reshuffle might be needed for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins are struggling with scoring, holding onto the final wild-card spot by one point after a 4-2 loss to San Jose.
David Pastrnak has seven points in eight games, but struggles persist with only two at 5-on-5 play; Elias Lindholm and Morgan Geekie have none.
Marco Sturm considers reshuffling lines as power play falters, now 4-for-28 in eight games, possibly pairing Pastrnak with Minten and Khusnutdinov.

title: Swiss student came to Boston to study rehab. Now he’s running for trauma survivors.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Alexis Cogne, a Swiss researcher at Harvard, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support trauma survivors.
Cogne aims to raise $10,000 for the Stepping Strong Center at Brigham and Women’s Hospital through marathon participation.
As a neurorehabilitation researcher, Cogne focuses on aiding recovery and independence for patients after severe injuries.

title: Fire destroys Newbury home, kills pets as family escapes
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A fire destroyed a Newbury home early Friday, leaving four residents unharmed but killing a dog and a cat.
The Newbury Fire Department received the fire report shortly before 1 a.m. and found the building heavily engulfed.
Mutual aid was provided by nearby towns, and an investigation is underway to determine the fire's origin and cause.

title: How a Cambridge home cook built a 1,000-member recipe club around local ingredients
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Andrew Little founded the Cambridge Recipe Club, focusing on local ingredients and fostering community among its 1,000 members.
The club, based in Cambridge, provides free recipes that utilize seasonal Massachusetts produce, distributed via a weekly newsletter.
Little seeks to expand the club by collaborating with members and local chefs, and possibly introducing meet-ups or a Slack channel.

title: Campbell to continue suit against Live Nation, as internal messages show employee mocked customers
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts AG Andrea Campbell will continue antitrust suit against Live Nation despite DOJ settlement.
Internal messages of Live Nation employee mocking customers revealed, raising scrutiny of company's practices.
DOJ agreement allows promoters at Live Nation-owned venues to choose ticket distribution for 50% of tickets.
